chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 163 of 2015 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Application in disposed of case 1. For orders on MA No.6261/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on MA No.6263/2025 (U/A) 3. For orders on MA No.6264/2025 (Ex./A) 4. For orders on MA No.6262/2025 (R/Surety) 5. For orders on MA No.6265/2025 (R/Surety) 06.11.2025 Mr. Aftab Ahmed Malano, Advocate for applicants..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1 & 2. Urgency is allowed. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4 & 5. These applications have been filed by applicants Abdul Kareem and Abdul Wahid seeking return of surety documents. As per contentions of learned Counsel, supported by report of Accountant of this Court, it appears that accused Ali Hassan alias Bhutto, Allah Wadhayo, Muhammad Saleem and Muhammad Hassan were granted interim pre-arrest bail by this Court vide order dated 09.03.2015, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/- each, for which applicant Abdul Kareem stood surety for three accused by depositing original solvency certificate No.08 dated 04.03.2015, issued by the Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Taluka Salehpat, whereas, applicant Abdul Wahid stood surety for remaining accused Muhammad Saleem by depositing original solvency certificate No.09 dated 06.03.2015, issued by the Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Taluka Salehpat. Thereafter, vide order dated 14.04.2016, the bail application to the extent accused Muhammad Hassan was converted into protective bail, which was granted for a period of seven days, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum Rs.50,000/-.

As per contentions of learned Counsel, supported by report of Accountant of this Court, it appears that accused Ali Hassan alias Bhutto, Allah Wadhayo, Muhammad Saleem and Muhammad Hassan were granted interim pre-arrest bail by this Court vide order dated 09.03.2015, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.1,00,000/- each, for which applicant Abdul Kareem stood surety for three accused by depositing original solvency certificate No.08 dated 04.03.2015, issued by the Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Taluka Salehpat, whereas, applicant Abdul Wahid stood surety for remaining accused Muhammad Saleem by depositing original solvency certificate No.09 dated 06.03.2015, issued by the Mukhtiarkar (Revenue), Taluka Salehpat. Thereafter, vide order dated 14.04.2016, the bail application to the extent accused Muhammad Hassan was converted into protective bail, which was granted for a period of seven days, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum Rs.50,000/-. As he was already on interim pre-arrest bail against the aforesaid furnished surety, it was ordered that he may remain on same surety by swearing fresh affidavit of his earlier surety. In the meanwhile, all the accused have been acquitted by the learned trial Court, as is evident from the order dated 02.09.2016, annexed with these applications. Cr. Bail App. No. S – 163 of 2015 Page 2 of 2 Mr. Muhammad Raza Katohar, learned Deputy Prosecutor General, present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and does not oppose these applications. Accordingly, both these applications are allowed. Office is directed to return the surety documents to the applicants after proper identification and verification, as per rules. J U D G E Abdul Basit
